description_long: This lamppost features a robust, rectangular stone or concrete base supporting two tall, cylindrical metal posts, each topped with a single lantern-style lamp head. The posts are adorned with a decorative, twisting serpent or vine motif that wraps around the shaft, adding a dramatic and ornate touch. The lamp heads are hexagonal with a slightly domed top and glass panels, reminiscent of Victorian or Gothic street lighting. The surface of the base and posts shows signs of weathering, with visible dirt, discoloration, and patina, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The overall color palette is a mix of dark, aged metal and light, stained stone, giving the lamppost a historic and slightly neglected appearance. This object would be well-suited for placement in parks, historic districts, or themed urban environments.
description: A tall, dual-post lamppost with ornate twisting serpent motifs and hexagonal lantern heads, set on a weathered stone base. The design is historic and decorative, with visible aging and patina.
description_view_0: Front view showing both posts and lamp heads, with the twisting decorative elements clearly visible and the weathered stone base in full view.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the base and the profile of the twisting motif, with one lamp head more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of both posts and lamp heads, with the decorative elements partially visible and the base's weathering more pronounced.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile of the lamppost, with the twisting motif and lamp head visible from a different angle.
attribute_lamp_head_design: Hexagonal lantern-style heads with domed tops and glass panels, reminiscent of Victorian or Gothic design.
